findings.description: Officials' names and titles duplicated on HTML page 1. 'PAT McCRORY Governor', 'DONALD R. VAN DER VAART Secretary', and 'TRACY DAVIS Director' appear twice: once in the letterhead header area and again as plain body text before the MEMORANDUM heading. Footer text is also inserted between the header and memo content.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows officials' names only in the upper-right header. HTML page 1 renders them in the header AND repeats them as standalone body text lines below a footer block and color bar, creating a duplicated reading experience.

findings.description: Repeated footer and decorative color bar inserted between the end of the memo outline (Section I–IV list) and the beginning of the History/Background section, disrupting the continuous reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF flows directly from the outline on page 1 to History/Background on page 2 with a simple page break. HTML inserts the full footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | …') and a decorative color bar between these content sections within the continuous document.

findings.description: A full-page-width embedded image (612pt wide) is inserted between body sections—between the document outline and the 'I. History/Background' heading—where no standalone image exists in the PDF at that position. This likely represents a duplicate page rendering (page header/footer or full-page image) placed mid-content alongside the same text already rendered as HTML.
findings.evidence:
HTML: appears immediately before

I. History/Background

. The PDF has no standalone image at that location; the content is purely text.

findings.description: The second embedded image has empty alt text (alt=""), marking it as decorative. If it is in fact a substantive image (e.g., a full-page rendering or a page-level graphic), the empty alt text provides no information. Additionally, the first image's alt text 'NC DEQ logo' omits the sub-text 'Energy, Mineral & Land Resources / ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' visible in the PDF letterhead logo area.
findings.evidence: HTML image 1: alt="NC DEQ logo". PDF letterhead includes the NC tree logo with 'Energy, Mineral & Land Resources / ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' beneath it. HTML image 2: alt="" with width 612pt inserted mid-content.
